op20_disclosures.account_size_assumption: qty=3 contracts (matching $1K paper account, ~$150-500 premium per contract at 13:xx for ATM puts)
op20_disclosures.simulator: simulator_real.py (OPRA cache — real fills, NOT Black-Scholes)
op20_disclosures.tp1_runner_note: Simulation used standard defaults (+30% TP1, +300% runner). Spec WATCH-ONLY knobs (+25% TP1, 1.5× runner) would exit earlier → displayed P&L may be slightly higher than actual WATCH-ONLY knob results.
op20_disclosures.strike_fallback_policy: If exact ITM-2 strike not in OPRA cache, nearest cached put strike used (documented per signal). Fallback strikes may differ from production engine behavior.
op20_disclosures.out_of_sample: N=4 signals over 16 months — sample too small for statistical significance. OP-21 gate requires 3+ live J-confirmed observations before any promotion.
op20_disclosures.concentration: All 4 signals fire between 13:35-14:05 ET (post-lunch window). Not a full-day setup.
op20_disclosures.worst_case: The one loss (2026-03-23 13:40) lost $0.60 SPY per scan. At $1K account with 3 contracts, a -6% stop on a $1.50 entry = -$27 per trade if stop hits immediately.
op20_disclosures.blow_up_scenario: Countertrend setup on BULL ribbon day — if bull momentum accelerates instead of reversing, stop fires quickly. Max loss = 6% premium × 3 contracts = ~3-6% of $1K account per trade.

signals.note: Strike fallback: fallback_from_544_to_540. TP1/runner knobs at standard defaults.
TP1/runner knobs used standard simulator defaults (+30% / 3×); spec WATCH-ONLY knobs (+25% / 1.5×) would exit earlier → more conservative
Strike fallback: fallback_from_662_to_660. TP1/runner knobs at standard defaults.
Strike fallback: fallback_from_643_to_645. TP1/runner knobs at standard defaults.
